definitions: A framework designed to simplify the creation of applications using large language models (LLMs).
An open-source library that provides tools and abstractions to chain together different components of AI applications, such as models, data sources, and APIs.
examples: LangChain enables developers to build powerful LLM applications.
Many new AI projects are leveraging LangChain for their backend.
The latest update to LangChain includes new integrations.
